solicitations.title: Request for Quotation for Inspections, Testing, Certifications, Repairs, Parts and Service to Bergkamp Asphalt Patcher Body and Trecan Snowmelter
solicitations.description:
This Request for Quotation (the “RFQ”) is an invitation by the City of Toronto (the “City”) to prospective Suppliers to submit bids for the non-exclusive supply and delivery of all labour, materials, tools, equipment, travel time, mileage, fuel and incidentals necessary to perform inspections, testing, certifications, repairs, parts and service to:

a) Bergkamp Asphalt Patcher Body
b) Trecan Snowmelter

as further described in Part 3 and the Contract (the “Deliverables”).

solicitations.title: Doc5274436439 - Request for Proposal for Road Allowance and Control System (RACS) Modernization
solicitations.description:
The City of Toronto (the “City”) is issuing this Negotiated Request for Proposal (RFP) to select a qualified Supplier to bid for a Road Allowance and Control System (RACS) Modernization. The City is looking for a supportable, extendable, accessible, scalable, and cost-effective COTS Proposed Solution that would address the current challenges and business objectives of the City.


 


RFX closing on August 24th @  12noon local Toronto time.


The City of Toronto does not post budget information. The opportunity amount does not reflect the City’s actual project estimate.

composite_awards.title: General Services Contracts for the replacement of watermains, water service connections, sanitary sewers and storm sewers related to the Stand Alone Watermain Replacement Program in accordance with specifications as required by Technical Services. The Tender Call allowed the City to select up to six bidding contractors to perform work over a 24-month term. The determination with the respect to the number of contractors selected took into consideration the lowest overall prices that represented the best value to the City. An engineering estimate was established prior to the closing of the Tender Call, which was also a determining factor in the selection process. Each contractor selected by the City will be required to execute a General Services Contract. Task Orders will then be issued by the City to the contractors for all or part of any work listed in the Tender Call. The selected Contractors will each be awarded at least one Task Order with a minimum value of $500,000.00. The City reserves the right to cancel the contract with a contractor at any time after the minimum contract value has been achieved. The maximum value of each General Services Contract will be determined by the number of Task Orders completed by each contractor. A maximum cumulative amount per contractor will not exceed $20 million, net of GST and HST Recoveries.
